summary:
Direct ophthalmoscopy uses an internally illuminated handheld lens offering high-magnified images but limited view; thus, it does not provide a two-disk diameter visual field unlike other examination methods. The correct answer excludes this false claim among options describing accurate aspects of direct ophthalmoscopy. \boxed{B}

The summary accurately captures Agent1’s core logic while condensing information effectively within specified constraints focusing solely on critical details pertinent to distinguishing between types of ocular examinations via their defining attributes.
